reviewMeta.subject: Brady A. Finnell — AI/ML Engineer
reviewMeta.purpose: Curate completed, marketable work for a public portfolio targeting AI/ML engineer and full-stack developer roles.
sources: Project upload corpus at /home/user/workspace/space_files (33 files listed by the project; 18 distinct files resolved on disk after de-duplication)
limitations: OneDrive search was attempted by the main agent and returned zero indexed results, so the uploaded project corpus (space_files) was treated as the authoritative content source.
Uploaded files are private project uploads. Their storage URLs are NOT hotlinked. Only public, license-clear artifacts were copied into assets/docs.
Brady's email address appears in the resume and the original splash page but is deliberately excluded from all page markup per instructions.
Several uploaded files are duplicate uploads (e.g. -1/-2 suffixed copies) and were de-duplicated for review.
reviewMeta.inclusionCriteria: Substantial, completed implementations or strong technical documentation that demonstrate marketable AI/ML or full-stack skills. Excluded: generic coursework, incomplete/outdated tutorials, personal/sensitive material, and duplicates.

included.title: RTS Multi-Agent Deep Q-Learning (Farmers & Fighters)
included.rationale: Strongest AI project. From-scratch multi-agent DQN: custom Gymnasium-style environment, MLP Q-network, experience replay, hand-tuned reward shaping, and training curves. Directly maps to resume's 'deep reinforcement agents from scratch' and PyTorch/RL strengths. Complete, documented README with demo GIFs and license.

included.title: Flask Advancing AI — Full-Stack Forum Platform
included.demoNote: Demo requires a database/OAuth backend; live demo action is intentionally gated behind the 'unavailable — demo' modal per portfolio spec.
included.rationale: Most substantial full-stack build. Google OAuth 2.0, role-based access control, salted SHA-256 hashing, token flows, REST API, Flask-Talisman security headers, unit tests, and Docker. Demonstrates secure architecture and full-stack breadth the resume emphasizes.

included.title: Training Management Calendar
included.demoNote: Requires Flask backend and database; live action gated behind demo modal.
included.rationale: Complete, self-directed Flask app with auth, CRUD events, category/section filtering, FullCalendar frontend, and CSV/PDF export (WeasyPrint). Shows practical full-stack product thinking and data export — relevant to enterprise workflow/tooling roles.

included.title: Marine Mental Strength (M2S) — AI Triage System Design
included.rationale: INCLUDED WITH CARE. This is an academic ML system-design report (a use-case proposal, not a deployed clinical product and not real patient data). It is technically strong: RAG with a dual-encoder bi-transformer retriever, reranker, metadata filtering, a MentalBERT safety governor, HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification, NER PII/PHI scrubbing, and a full system architecture diagram. It demonstrates responsible-AI, privacy, and ML system-design skill. Presented as an academic design proposal with explicit framing; no individuals are identified, no sensitive personal data is present, and the topic is handled professionally. This satisfies the instruction to include the USMC mental-health project only if professionally appropriate, anonymized, and technically strong.

included.title: ShopSphere — Scalable Database Architecture (CSCI360)
included.rationale: Included as a documentation artifact. Complete, well-argued database-systems design (ShopSphere e-commerce): snowflake warehouse schema, ETL, PostgreSQL row-level concurrency control, parameterized-query API, RBAC + SSL/TLS + hashing, and a distributed horizontal-scaling plan. Backs the resume's PostgreSQL/MySQL/SQLite database-design claims. Note: the project files list a separate 'Database Diagram.pdf', but that file did not resolve on disk in space_files — only the description docx and queries xlsx were present. To avoid fabricating a missing asset, the project is presented as a scrollable, non-editable text summary (built with textContent) rather than an embedded PDF. This satisfies the wireframe's 'Word document: non-editable scrollable window' behavior.

excluded.title: Asteroids (Pygame)
excluded.reason: Boot.dev guided tutorial project with an empty README; learning exercise rather than an original, marketable build. Excluded to keep the portfolio focused on substantial work.

excluded.title: Flask Blog/To-Do
excluded.reason: Small learning project ('made while learning Flask', CSCI325 week 7). Superseded by the far more substantial Flask-Advancing-AI and calendar apps. Excluded as introductory coursework.

excluded.title: Flask Tutorial
excluded.reason: Explicitly a 'School Tutorial' follow-along; not original work. Excluded.

excluded.title: webflyx
excluded.reason: Described as 'git training repo'; no substantive project content. Excluded.

excluded.title: web_portfolio
excluded.reason: This is the destination repository for this very site (contains only a LICENSE at review time). Not a portfolio item.

excluded.title: Analysis of Current LLMs (CSCI484)
excluded.reason: Course essay analyzing then-current LLMs. Time-sensitive product claims would date quickly and conflict with the site's 'no time-sensitive model claims' rule. Excluded to avoid stale content; the ANN report is likewise generic coursework.

excluded.title: Restaurant App Plan (CSCI340)
excluded.reason: A planning/design deck for a coursework app with no shipped implementation. Not a completed build; excluded in favor of finished projects.

excluded.title: Interactive Fitness Prototype (CSCI315 Web UI)
excluded.reason: UI prototype descriptions and a large prototype PDF from a UX course. Prototype-only coursework, not a working application; excluded to prioritize substantial implementations.

excluded.title: HTML Quiz Write-Up (CSCI325)
excluded.reason: Introductory HTML coursework write-up (also a ~10MB single doc). Generic beginner material; excluded.

excluded.title: MATH302 Statistics — Executive Summary & Data Analysis
excluded.reason: General-education statistics coursework, not AI/ML or software engineering. Excluded as non-marketable for the target roles.

excluded.title: Assorted general-education coursework
excluded.reason: STEM185 timeline, ENGL235 linguistics, IRLS200 information-literacy essays, PSYC304 perspective paper, COMM120 digital-literacy deck: general-education assignments unrelated to AI/ML or software engineering. Excluded.
